-r: Recursive — applies the operation to all files and subdirectories
-R: Recursive — applies the operation to all files and subdirectories
--recursive: Recursive — applies the operation to all files and subdirectories
-f: Force — skip confirmation prompts and ignore errors
--force: Force — skip confirmation prompts and ignore errors
-i: Interactive — prompt before each action or action on existing files
-v: Verbose — display detailed output of what is being done
-n: Number lines or dry-run mode (depends on command context)
--dry-run: Dry run — simulate the operation without making changes
-h: Human-readable — display sizes in KB, MB, GB instead of bytes
--human-readable: Human-readable — display sizes in a readable format
-l: Long format — display detailed listing with permissions, size, and date
-a: All — include hidden files (those starting with a dot)
--all: All — include hidden files or show all entries
-d: Directory — list directory entries instead of their contents
--directory: Directory — treat directories as plain files
-t: Sort by modification time, newest first
-S: Sort by file size, largest first
-u: Sort by access time or show access time
-c: Sort by change time or show change time
-p: Append / indicator to directories, or create parent directories
-m: Set file mode permissions (chmod), or comma-separated list
-e: Execute a command or use an expression
--directory: Change to a specified directory before performing operations
-O: Write to standard output, keep remote files unchanged
-b: Make a backup of each existing destination file
-u: Only copy when source is newer than destination
--update: Only copy files that are newer than their destination
--preserve: Preserve file attributes such as timestamps and permissions
-w: Limit output width, or set warning options
-k: Keep original files (do not delete after compression)
--append: Append files to an existing archive
-t: List contents of an archive (tar) or test archive integrity
--same-permissions: Extract files with the same permissions as archived
-e: Stop after first match (grep) or exit on error (bash)
--regexp: Use a regular expression for pattern matching
--ignore-case: Ignore case distinctions in pattern matching
-l: Print only names of files with matching lines
-L: Print names of files with no matching lines
--files-without-match: Only print filenames with no matches
-c: Print count of matching lines per file
-n: Print line numbers with output (grep) or dry-run (make)
--line-number: Print line numbers with matching output
-o: Print only the matching part of lines
--only-matching: Print only the matching portion of lines
--invert-match: Print lines that do NOT match the pattern
--word-regexp: Force pattern to match only whole words
--line-regexp: Force pattern to match only whole lines
-A: Print N lines of context after each match
--after-context: Show N lines of context after matches
-B: Print N lines of context before each match
--before-context: Show N lines of context before matches
-C: Print N lines of context before and after each match
--context: Show N lines of context before and after matches
-H: Print the filename for each match
--no-filename: Do not print filename with matches
--null: Print null byte after filename (for use with xargs)
-maxdepth: Limit find search to N levels of directory depth
-mindepth: Skip the first N levels of directories in find
-iname: Search for files by name, case-insensitive (find)
-type: Search for files of a specific type (f=file, d=dir, l=link)
-size: Search for files of a specific size (find)
-mtime: Search for files modified N days ago (find)
-atime: Search for files accessed N days ago (find)
-ctime: Search for files changed N days ago (find)
-newer: Find files newer than a reference file
-perm: Search for files with specific permissions
-user: Search for files owned by a specific user
-group: Search for files belonging to a specific group
-exec: Execute a command on each found file
-print: Print the full file name (default find action)
-print0: Print filenames separated by null characters
-k: Kill signal — specify the signal to send (kill command)
-9: SIGKILL — forcefully terminate a process immediately
-15: SIGTERM — gracefully ask a process to terminate
-HUP: SIGHUP — hangup signal, often used to reload config
--signal: Specify the signal to send to a process
-a: All processes or all network connections
-u: Show processes for a specific user
-x: Show processes without a controlling terminal (ps)
-e: Show every process on the system
-aux: Show all processes with full details (common ps usage)
-s: Summary of totals (du) or silent mode
--summarize: Display only the total for each argument
--max-depth: Display totals for directories up to specified depth
--apparent-size: Show apparent sizes rather than disk usage
--print-type: Print the type of each filesystem
-i: Show inode information instead of block usage
-H: Print sizes in human-readable powers of 1000
-a: Write counts for all files, not just directories (du)
-m: Create as directory with set permissions (mkdir)
-v: Print a message for each created directory
--recursive: Apply recursively to all files and subdirectories
--reference: Use permissions/ownership from a reference file
-c: Report only when a change is made
--changes: Report only when a change is made
--from: Change only when current owner/group matches (chown)
--no-preserve-root: Do not treat root directory specially (DANGEROUS!)
--preserve-root: Refuse to operate recursively on root (safety measure)
--no-dereference: Affect symbolic link itself, not what it points to
-h: Affect symbolic links instead of referenced files
-l: Use logical time zone for date display
-d: Display the time specified in STRING
--date: Specify a date/time string to display
-u: Update a package (apt) or sort by access time (ls)
--only-upgrade: Only upgrade packages, do not install new ones
--purge: Remove packages and all their configuration files
-s: Simulate the operation without making changes (apt)
--simulate: Simulate, do not make actual changes
--no-download: Disable downloading, use only cached packages
--fix-broken: Attempt to fix broken package dependencies
--fix-missing: Ignore missing packages when fixing dependencies
--debug: Enable debug mode with extra output
--trace: Enable trace mode for detailed execution information
--no-progress: Do not show a progress bar
--port: Specify the port to connect to
-o: Specify SSH option in key=value format
--bind-address: Bind to a specific local IP address
-O: Save output to a specific filename (wget)
-P: Save files to a specific directory prefix (wget)
--directory-prefix: Save downloaded files to this directory
--limit-rate: Limit download speed to a specified rate
--tries: Set number of retries for failed downloads
--timeout: Set the network timeout in seconds
--user-agent: Identify as a specific browser or client
--max-redirs: Set maximum number of redirects to follow
-H: Pass custom headers to the server (curl)
--header: Pass extra header in the request
--data: Send data in a POST request
-u: Specify username and password for authentication (curl)
--insecure: Allow connections to SSL sites without certificates (DANGEROUS)
-o: Write output to a file instead of stdout (curl)
--output: Write to file instead of stdout
--write-out: Define output format after request completion
--cookie-jar: Save cookies to a specified file
--cookie: Send cookies from a file or string
--user-agent: Identify the client to the server
--proxy: Use a proxy for the request
--cacert: Use specified CA bundle for SSL verification
--capath: Use CA certificates in specified directory
--noproxy: Comma-separated list of hosts to bypass proxy
-j: Enable job control or junk cookies (wget)
--no-cookies: Do not read or write cookies
--follow-tags: Follow only links in specified HTML tags (wget)
--domains: Restrict following of links to specified domains (wget)
--span-hosts: Enable spanning across hosts when recursive (wget)
--page-requisites: Download all resources needed to display the page
--mirror: Turn on options for mirroring a site (wget)
--adjust-extension: Save HTML/CSS documents with proper extensions (wget)
--no-parent: Do not ascend to the parent directory (wget)
--random-wait: Wait a random interval between downloads (wget)
-nd: Do not create directories when downloading recursively (wget)
-np: Do not ascend to the parent directory (wget)
